# Pixir

Pixir is an Elixir/OTP harness for supervised coding-agent sessions, subagents,
workflows, lifecycle state, and replayable local evidence behind any presenter.

## Canonical Site

- https://pixir.dev/

## Install

```bash
mix escript.install hex pixir
pixir doctor --json
pixir acp
```

## Product Boundary

- Supported public surface: CLI and ACP runtime.
- Daily-driver path: ACP clients, including local T3 Code dogfood.
- Positioning: Codex is an operator/meta-orchestrator and auditor; Zed, T3Code,
  and other ACP clients are UI/client/presenters; Pixir is the supervised local
  runtime for bounded agent work.
- Cache stance: Pixir treats WebSocket continuation, stable prompt prefixes, and
  minimal per-turn payloads as cache-friendly runtime choices, but cache hits must be
  proven by Provider usage evidence such as cached token counts.
- Not yet: finished Pi-style TUI replacement, packaged T3 provider, MCP server,
  stable public Elixir API, hosted SLA software, or multi-provider marketplace.

## Primary Links

- Hex package: https://hex.pm/packages/pixir
- HexDocs: https://pixir.hexdocs.pm/readme.html
- Source: https://github.com/Ranvier-Technologies/pixir-harness
